title: quercus iberica var. kozlowskyi
intro: Quercus iberica var. kozlowskyi, also known as the Caucasian or Turkish oak, is a variety of the Quercus iberica species, which is a type of evergreen tree native to the mountainous regions of the Caucasus. The plant is rich in tannins and polyphenols, making it valuable for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus iberica var. kozlowskyi include its potential to protect against cardiovascular disease, improve cognitive function, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and decoctions, are traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
